metadata.title: Palbociclib Therapy - 28 day
metadata.indication: Palbociclib in combination with an aromatase inhibitor or fulvestrant for eligible HR-positive, HER2-negative locally advanced or metastatic breast cancer.
metadata.partial_assessment_note: Only entered values are assessed; omitted clinical domains remain explicitly unassessed.

clinical_governance.prescriptive_authority: Treatment plan must be initiated by a Consultant Medical Oncologist.
clinical_governance.dose_modification_note: Any dose modification should be discussed with a Consultant.
clinical_governance.disclaimer: Decision-support encoding of the current NCCP source. It does not replace the NCCP regimen, clinical judgement, local policy, prescribing review or pharmacy verification.

fever_or_infection.label: Fever ≥38.5°C and/or infection with grade 3 neutropenia
fever_or_infection.help_text: Grade 3 neutropenia with fever ≥38.5°C and/or infection follows the febrile/infected neutropenia dose-interruption pathway.

prolonged_or_recurrent_grade3.label: Prolonged (>1 week) or recurrent grade 3 neutropenia on Day 1

nonhaem_toxicity_grade.label: Worst current non-haematological toxicity (CTCAE)

options.label: Grade 0 — no toxicity
options.description: No clinical or laboratory evidence of non-haematological toxicity.

options.label: Grade 1 — mild
options.description: Mild or asymptomatic; clinical observation or diagnostic review only; intervention generally not indicated.

options.label: Grade 2 — moderate
options.description: Moderate symptoms or abnormality; minimal, local or non-invasive intervention may be indicated; limits age-appropriate instrumental activities of daily living.

options.label: Grade 3 — severe
options.description: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalisation or prolongation of hospitalisation may be indicated; disabling; limits self-care activities of daily living.

nonhaem_toxicity_grade.assessment_guidance: Grade the named toxicity using its CTCAE definition. Record functional impact, treatment required and whether it persists despite optimal medical management.

nonhaem_persists_despite_treatment.label: Grade ≥3 toxicity persists despite medical treatment

renal_band.label: Renal function category

options.label: CrCl ≥15 mL/min — no protocol dose adjustment required
CrCl <15 mL/min / end-stage renal disease — limited evidence; specialist review

renal_band.assessment_guidance: Use the most recent clinically appropriate CrCl. The source protocol does not require finer dose tiers for CrCl ≥15 mL/min.

output_templates.proceed: No encoded contraindication or hold criterion was triggered. Complete normal prescribing and pharmacy checks.
output_templates.coverage_gap: Only entered values were assessed; unentered clinically relevant fields remain unassessed.

action.recommendation: Withhold until ANC ≥1.0 and platelets ≥50 ×10⁹/L, then resume at the next lower dose.

rules.explanation: Withhold until ANC ≥1.0 and platelets ≥50 ×10⁹/L, then resume at the next lower dose.

action.recommendation: Withhold until ANC ≥1.0 and platelets ≥50 ×10⁹/L, then resume at the next lower dose.

rules.explanation: Withhold until ANC ≥1.0 and platelets ≥50 ×10⁹/L, then resume at the next lower dose.

action.recommendation: Withhold until ANC ≥1.0 and platelets ≥50 ×10⁹/L, then resume at the next lower dose.

rules.explanation: Withhold until ANC ≥1.0 and platelets ≥50 ×10⁹/L, then resume at the next lower dose.

action.recommendation: Day 1 grade 3 neutropenia: withhold and repeat FBC within 1 week; resume at same dose after ANC ≥1.0 and platelets ≥50.

rules.explanation: Day 1 grade 3 neutropenia: withhold and repeat FBC within 1 week; resume at same dose after ANC ≥1.0 and platelets ≥50.

action.recommendation: Day 1 grade 3 thrombocytopenia: withhold and repeat FBC within 1 week; resume at same dose after recovery.

rules.explanation: Day 1 grade 3 thrombocytopenia: withhold and repeat FBC within 1 week; resume at same dose after recovery.

action.recommendation: On Day 15 of cycles 1–2, continue current dose for grade 3 cytopenia and repeat FBC on Day 22.

rules.explanation: On Day 15 of cycles 1–2, continue current dose for grade 3 cytopenia and repeat FBC on Day 22.

action.recommendation: Consider one dose-level reduction for prolonged or recurrent grade 3 neutropenia.

rules.explanation: Consider one dose-level reduction for prolonged or recurrent grade 3 neutropenia.

action.recommendation: Withhold until recovery to grade ≤1, or grade 2 if not a safety risk, then resume at the next lower dose.

rules.explanation: Withhold until recovery to grade ≤1, or grade 2 if not a safety risk, then resume at the next lower dose.

action.recommendation: Use 60% of the original dose in Child-Pugh C.

rules.explanation: Use 60% of the original dose in Child-Pugh C.
Severe end-stage renal impairment or dialysis requires consultant and oncology-pharmacy review because evidence is limited.

action.recommendation: If a further dose reduction below 75 mg/day is required, discontinue treatment.

rules.explanation: If a further dose reduction below 75 mg/day is required, discontinue treatment.

supportive_care.supportive_medications_label: Low-emetogenic-risk oral targeted therapy antiemetic script
premedication_summary: Routine premedication is not usually required.
other_supportive_care: Provide patient-held advice on fever, infection and neutropenia.
Check CYP3A interactions and avoid grapefruit products where applicable.

ctcae_standard.reference: CTCAE grading as cited by the NCCP source protocol
ctcae_standard.display_requirement: Every grade-based input includes toxicity-specific criteria and practical assessment guidance. Do not select a grade from the number alone.
